title: About Us
body_html:
About iComply Supplys

iComply Supplys is the online trade and retail supply arm of iComply Property Services, based in Offerton, Stockport. We supply fire detection equipment, emergency lighting products, batteries, cable and related electrical trade goods across the UK mainland — supported by practical site experience, not just a warehouse catalogue.

Who we are

iComply Property Services works with landlords, facilities managers, contractors and commercial clients on fire, security and compliance-related systems. Through iComply Supplys we make the same trade-focused product ranges available online: control panels, detectors, call points, sounders and beacons, interfaces, power supplies, fire cable accessories and emergency lighting.

What we stand for

  • Trade-ready stock: Conventional and addressable equipment from established manufacturers used on real UK sites.
  • Clear guidance: Product information written for installers, electricians and responsible persons — not marketing fluff.
  • B2B and B2C: Open website checkout for one-off needs, plus trade accounts for regular contractors and service companies.
  • Local accountability: A named UK business you can phone or email — Stockport-based, serving customers nationwide.

Our address

iComply Property Services / iComply Supplys
17 Woodlands Park Road
Offerton, Stockport
SK2 5DE

Telephone: 07517 806082
Email: icomplypropertyservices@gmail.com

Beyond supply

Need installation, servicing or certification as well as equipment? Ask us about service packages from iComply Property Services — including fire alarm, emergency lighting and related compliance support for commercial and multi-occupancy buildings.

Contact us · Open a trade account · Browse products

title: Contact
body_html:
Contact iComply Supplys

Questions about stock, compatibility, delivery or trade pricing? We’re happy to help — whether you’re ordering a single detector base or equipping a full fire system project.

Get in touch

iComply Supplys / iComply Property Services
17 Woodlands Park Road
Offerton, Stockport
SK2 5DE

  • Phone: 07517 806082
  • Email: icomplypropertyservices@gmail.com

What to include in your enquiry

  • Order number (if you already ordered)
  • Product codes or links for the items you need
  • Panel/protocol type (e.g. conventional, Apollo, Hochiki, Kentec, Advanced, C-TEC)
  • Delivery postcode for carriage estimates
  • Whether you need a trade account or site delivery

Opening hours

We aim to respond to emails and calls during normal UK working hours, Monday to Friday. Urgent trade despatch enquiries are prioritised where stock allows.

Trade accounts

For regular contractor pricing and account ordering, see our Trade Accounts page or email us with your company name, VAT number and typical monthly requirements.

Returns & delivery

Delivery information · Returns: unused goods within 14 days with authorisation — full details in our store policies.

title: Trade Accounts
body_html:
Trade Accounts

iComply Supplys supports electricians, fire alarm engineers, FM contractors, electrical wholesalers’ customers and service companies who need reliable access to fire and emergency lighting equipment.

Why open a trade account?

  • Trade-focused pricing on stocked lines (subject to approval and volume)
  • Faster re-ordering for panels, devices, bases, batteries and cable accessories
  • Support with product selection against common UK protocols and panel ranges
  • Options for consolidated despatch and site deliveries on larger orders
  • Invoice/pro-forma workflows where credit terms are agreed

How to apply

  1. Email icomplypropertyservices@gmail.com with the subject line Trade Account Application.
  2. Include: company name, trading address, VAT number (if registered), contact name, phone, email, and a short note on the products you typically buy.
  3. We will confirm account setup, pricing approach and payment terms (card/pro-forma by default; credit only where expressly agreed).

Trade terms summary

  • Payment: due at order or on pro-forma unless written credit terms apply.
  • Delivery: UK mainland standard and express options; collection by arrangement from Stockport (Offerton).
  • Returns: unused, resaleable stock within 14 days with Returns Authorisation; restocking may apply on opened non-faulty trade returns. Special-order items are non-returnable unless faulty.
  • Pricing: guide prices online may differ from live trade quotes on project quantities — always confirm large orders before committing to site programmes.

Competency reminder

Fire detection and emergency lighting equipment must be designed, installed and maintained by competent persons in line with relevant British Standards and fire safety legislation. Account supply does not include design responsibility unless separately contracted.

Contact

iComply Property Services, 17 Woodlands Park Road, Offerton, Stockport SK2 5DE
Tel: 07517 806082 · Email: icomplypropertyservices@gmail.com

title: Delivery Information
body_html:
Delivery Information

We despatch fire and electrical trade equipment across the UK mainland. Below is a plain-English guide to how shipping works with iComply Supplys.

Delivery coverage

  • Included at standard checkout rates: England, Wales and mainland Scotland (subject to carrier coverage for your postcode).
  • On request / quoted separately: Northern Ireland, Highlands & Islands, Isle of Man, Channel Islands and other remote locations.

Service options

ServiceTypical useIndicative timing after despatch
CollectionLocal trade pickupBy arrangement — Stockport / Offerton area
StandardEconomy tracked parcelsUsually 2–4 working days
ExpressNext working day (before cut-off, in stock)Next working day to most mainland postcodes

Exact prices are calculated at checkout by weight and destination. Bulky items (control panels, cable, bulk device orders) may ship as multi-parcel or pallet consignments.

Processing & cut-offs

In-stock lines ordered on a working day before our despatch cut-off are typically processed the same or next working day. Weekend and bank holiday orders are processed on the next working day. Special-order equipment is confirmed with an estimated lead time before we take non-refundable deposits where applicable.

Tracking & access

Tracking links are sent by email when your order ships. Provide a mobile number for courier notifications. For site deliveries, include gate codes, site contact and any restricted access times in the order notes.

Damage, shortages & failed delivery

  • Inspect parcels on arrival and report transit damage or shortages within 48 hours with photos.
  • Failed delivery or long waiting-time charges from carriers may be recharged on trade site deliveries.
  • Change-of-address after despatch may not be possible once the carrier label is created.

Collection

Free collection is available by prior arrangement when stock is reserved. Contact us on 07517 806082 or icomplypropertyservices@gmail.com to book.

Returns

Unused goods may be returned within 14 days with a Returns Authorisation. See our refund policy at checkout for full terms.

Address: iComply Property Services, 17 Woodlands Park Road, Offerton, Stockport SK2 5DE

title: Fire Alarm Systems Guide
body_html:
Fire Alarm Systems Guide

A practical overview for contractors, electricians and building managers buying fire detection equipment from iComply Supplys. This guide is general information only — always design and install to the current edition of BS 5839-1 (commercial) or other applicable standards, manufacturer instructions, and fire risk assessment requirements.

Conventional vs addressable

  • Conventional systems: Devices are wired in zones. The panel indicates which zone is in fire/fault, not the individual device. Often used in smaller premises and simple layouts.
  • Addressable systems: Each device has an address on a loop. The panel shows device location text, supports advanced cause-and-effect, and is typical for larger or more complex buildings.

Core components we supply

  • Control panels — conventional and addressable panels (e.g. ranges associated with Kentec, Advanced, C-TEC and similar trade lines).
  • Detectors — smoke, heat and multi-sensor heads for appropriate environments (optical smoke for most rooms; heat for kitchens/plant; multi-sensor where specified).
  • Manual call points (MCPs) — break-glass or resettable units at exits and storey exits as required by the design.
  • Sounders & beacons / VADs — audible and visual alarm devices to achieve required sound pressure levels and visual coverage.
  • Bases & mounting — standard, diode and isolator bases matched to the detector family.
  • Interfaces & modules — input/output units, zone monitors and loop interfaces for plant shutdown, door release and third-party signals.
  • Batteries & power supplies — standby batteries and 24V PSUs sized for the system load and standby duration.
  • Fire cable & accessories — enhanced/standard fire-resistant cable, glands and ancillaries for compliant wiring methods.

Protocol compatibility

Addressable detectors, call points and modules must match the panel’s protocol (for example Apollo or Hochiki device families are not interchangeable with every panel). Always check panel documentation and our product descriptions before ordering. If in doubt, email us the panel model and we will help identify compatible devices.

Emergency lighting

Fire alarm systems are often specified alongside emergency lighting. Escape route and open-area luminaires should be designed to BS 5266 and the building’s fire strategy. Browse our emergency lighting products for maintained/non-maintained luminaires and exit signage — commissioning and monthly/annual testing remain the duty holder’s responsibility.

Batteries and standby

Standby batteries are consumable. Replace on manufacturer schedule or when capacity tests fail. Use the correct voltage, capacity (Ah) and terminal type; never mix old and new sets on the same charger without following manufacturer guidance.

Buying checklist

  1. Confirm conventional vs addressable and the panel manufacturer/protocol.
  2. List device quantities from the design drawings or cause-and-effect schedule.
  3. Match bases, isolators and modules to the detector/panel family.
  4. Size batteries and PSUs for alarm load + standby duration required by the standard/spec.
  5. Allow spare devices and call points for spares holding on larger sites.
  6. Plan cable type (standard vs enhanced) per the specification and building type.

Service & maintenance

Equipment supply is only part of compliance. Systems require competent installation, commissioning, user training and planned maintenance. Ask about service packages from iComply Property Services if you need inspection, servicing or certification support.

Need help selecting products?

Contact iComply Supplys with your panel type, device list or drawings:

Email: icomplypropertyservices@gmail.com
17 Woodlands Park Road, Offerton, Stockport SK2 5DE
